body.title: Surface slow-query report proactively on boot (PROC_SLOW_QUERY_REPORT habit)
body.result: 00a0599b — boot_surface_slow_queries + main.py wire (post BOOT_INVENTORY). Top-5 by worst-elapsed pushed to ring as db_diagnostic. Silent on failure. 5 tests.
body.description:
db_queries.log is actively collecting slow-query data (55k entries, 10MB, updating live) and analyze_slow_queries() tool exists, but findings are never surfaced proactively — only on-demand when explicitly asked. Register a PROC_SLOW_QUERY_REPORT habit that fires on boot or daily, calling analyze_slow_queries() and pushing the top-5 offenders to ring_memory. The T-slow-query-analysis done ticket noted this habit but it was never actually registered in code or DB.

**Affected files:** wild_igor/igor/tools/slow_query.py (add boot hook or habit registration). No main.py touch needed — use habit/scheduler infrastructure instead.
**Design rules:** no-sqlite: reads db_queries.log (flat file), writes to ring_memory (Postgres, existing table). oop-first: none apply (single function registration). docs-in-code: none apply (slow_query.py is not a load-bearing subsystem file). test-plan-or-why-not: applies.
**Scope boundary:** IN — register proactive habit, surface top-5 to ring_memory on boot. OUT — automated ticketing from findings, Slack/Discord surfacing, threshold alerting.
**Test plan:** Unit test — mock analyze_slow_queries() output, assert ring_memory receives a slow_query_report event with correct top offenders on boot trigger.
